mechanics.text:
EQUIPMENT
Pulse Laser Rifle: Unlike other laser weapons the pulse Iaser rifle has yet to gain widespread acceptance The weapon' high cost and scarcity make it weapon of the wealthy:
rating accounts for built-in recoil compensation as well as stat ic mount; no additional recoil compensation can be added;
Lasers While far more expensive than their machine gun brethren_ heawy support-class asers can deliver an incredible amount of firepower:  Conversely; they  require incredible amount power: Even the heaviest of portable power packs provide them only enough charge for few shots Tne support lasers do how- ever; have special port that allows them plug directly into vehicle 0r building power system Support Laser: Like the support machine gun the support Iaser is too heavy and bulky for a infantryman to carry: However; can be mounted on battle- armor troopers battlesuits.
BUPPORT WEAPONS Usually heavy and expensive , support weapons give foot soldiers fighting chance against tanks, BattleMechs ad other heavily armored obstacles These weapons include machine guns, lasers_ particle beams and rockets They are large and capable of inflicting great damage_
Grenade Launchers The common foot soldier calls upon this weapon get out sticky situation Capable firing number of different muni: tions the grenade launcher is an anti-armor weapon demolition and portable artillery piece rolled into one Most militaries issue at least one grenade launcher per infantry squad. Compact Grenade Launcher: This the most common launcher use and comes in two models The first resembles large pistol with a extendable shoulder stock. The second is mounted underneath the barrel rifle and consists of just parrel and trigger block The launcher holds round; and t0 reload_ the shooter mmst spend Simple Action physically open  the preech and insert new round_ Though compact grenade launchers use special grenades; for game purposes their effects are the same as minigrenades (below); damage weight and cost are identical. However; these grenades cannot thrown by nand
EXPLOSIVES High explosives are frequently used in combat: Civilians also use them for demolition old buildings removal of rock from work sites and even as spectacular light and sound shows during celebrations Strictly regulated mosi civilized worlds, high explosives are easy obtain for those with the coreci licenses clearances Many different explosive compounds are available_ each vitn different price tag Some_ like grenades_ are available t0 the military only: Others _ like flares can be found just about anywhere
Grenades Grenades have been an intantryman most portable port weapon for centuries. Small and lightweight; they are capa: ble of devastating damage- grenades are usually thrown_ they are also one of the few weapons that rely on both the skill and the strength of the user. There are thousands of different grenades, though they fit into just few main categories and all share tne same game mechanics All grenades cause blast damage (p. 118) ad can be thrown over obstacles as a indi- rect (p; 119) attack. Grenade: The standard grenade the largest and most commonly issued of high-explosive grenades Minlgrenade: Slightly more than half the mass Of the stan: dard grenade this nas slightly ess powerful charge. It is however. preferred soldiers whose job itis clear out bunkers and other confined spaces as tne blast is less Vikely injure the thrower Microgrenade: Tiny in comparison to its bigger brothers_ this iS rarely used by military personnel . Undercover agents and criminals looking for potent and concealable weapon are the microgrenade' biggest fans
Mlssile Launchers Personal missile launchers, though bulky, are cheap; OCCU rate and effective ati-armor weapons Light AntfVehicle Weapon (LAW): This inexpensive and dis posable one shot weapon offers infantry tighting chance against armored adversaries; iS lighter and easier to fire than the SRM launcher; making the most widely used anti-vehicle weapon_ SRM Launcher: The heavy SRM launcher fires the same ammunition as vehicle and Mech-class SRM launchers It is very encumbering: and each missile weighs in at 10 kilograms; virtually requiring soldiers to set up in a emplacement and have vehicle close at hand to haul its ammunition;
Machine Guns Dependable inexpensive and easily repaired, heavy machine euns are the support weapon of choice for infantry- men; Portable Machine Gun: Light enough to be carried by sin- gle trooper; can be found in almost every infantry squad alongside the grenade launcher: consumes horrendous amount of ammunition requiring virtually constant supply (carried either in vehicle by other squad members}, Support Machlne Gun: Mounted on vehicles ad emplace ment turrets this is too massive for single trooper t0 carry: extraordinary range tor - slug throwing weapon and fires round that can damage even heavily armored vehicles The recoil
Demolitlons Demolition charges are designed to cut through or destroy buildings ad vehicles_ The explosions themselves are relative ly contained, as any blast energy not directed at the target' considered wasted energy: As such_ the AP and damage reduced for every half-meter from the point of impact; rather than every meter as with other blast weapons,
